critic_raw: The design requires '2-3 large hospital systems' as data sources. With only 2-3 sites, the institutional variation is limited — the held-out test institution is one of only 2-3 sites, representing a narrow range of institutional diversity. Findings may not generalize beyond this specific set of institutions. Additionally, if the held-out test institution is chosen based on convenience or data availability, it may not be a random sample of institutions, introducing selection bias in the generalization test.
defender_raw: Round 1: The scope section explicitly acknowledges: 'This design establishes whether text embeddings improve subtype prediction on held-out future data across multiple sites. It does not establish the mechanism or whether the text encodes clinically appropriate information.' The limitation to 2-3 sites is correctly framed as a scope constraint, not a methodological flaw. The experiment provides evidence about whether multimodal fusion helps across the specific sites studied. For broader generalization claims, more sites would be needed. The design is honest about this. On institutional selection bias: the design uses a multi-center archive with 3-5 years of data — within these institutions, the held-out test institution should be selected based on data availability and size, not performance optimization. This is the standard approach in clinical AI.

rounds.critic_followup: If the held-out institution is the smallest (or has most different protocols), the test may be either too easy (similar distribution) or too hard (extreme distribution shift). How does the design ensure the held-out institution is a representative generalization challenge?
rounds.defender_followup: The per-site performance analysis (per-site and per-time-period macro-F1) characterizes how different the held-out institution is from the training distribution. If the held-out institution is too similar (similar staining protocols, same scanner), this would be reflected in high test macro-F1 with low distribution shift — a positive finding. If it is very different (extreme protocol difference), lower macro-F1 is an honest result about generalization limits. The design reports this transparently. The choice of which institution to hold out should be documented and justified in the experimental report — typically the largest or most diverse institution is held out for the most challenging generalization test.

adjudication_raw: Two concerns: limited institutional diversity (2-3 sites) and held-out institution selection bias. Both addressed: scope is explicitly limited to the studied institutions; per-site analysis characterizes distribution shift; institution selection should be documented. Verdict: defense_wins.
